summary: Smoky first set with standout 'Don't Ease Me In', fiery second half
blurb: The show delivers a solid first set highlighted by a soulful 'Don’t Ease Me In' and a classic China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider. The second set erupts with a scorching Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain, tight jams and Phil’s booming bass lines. Overall flow is smooth and the band is locked in.
review: July 8, 1981 at Kiel Auditorium captures the Dead in a high‑energy mode. The opening half of the night feels “smoking” despite a few average moments; Jerry’s guitar is restrained early on but really kicks up on Sugar Magnolia and U.S. Blues, delivering big solos that lift the set. A highlight for many listeners is the soulful rendition of ‘Don’t Ease Me In’, while the second set shines with a blistering Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain, a tight China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, and an effortless Sugar Magnolia that drives the crowd wild. Phil’s bass “bombs” punctuate the jams, Brent Mydland’s keyboards stay solid throughout, and the rhythm section keeps a propulsive pulse. The performance is consistently praised across recordings, making this a must‑listen for anyone hunting a strong mid‑summer 1981 show.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four of the five recordings are described with positive sentiment, highlighting classic sequences (China Cat > Rider, Scarlet > Fire), strong Phil ‘bombs’, and solid overall flow. One mixed review notes poor tape quality but still calls the show solid. No negative performance comments appear, so a strong positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with excellent, crystal‑clear audio that captures the band’s tight playing and dynamic set without the crowd noise issues of audience tapes.

key_highlights: Classic China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue
Fiery Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain sequence
Standout ‘Don’t Ease Me In’ performance in the first set
High‑energy Sugar Magnolia and U.S. Blues closing the second set

band_performance.Jerry: Starts a bit restrained in the first set but amps up on Sugar Magnolia, U.S. Blues and delivers big solos into Big River later, showing his trademark fire.
band_performance.Phil: Lays down frequent ‘bombs’, especially during China Cat Sunflower, adding power and drive to the jams.
band_performance.Bob: Muffled early on but comes alive later with a sweet vocal on Saint Of Circumstance and solid guitar work throughout.
band_performance.Brent: Provides great, steady keyboard textures that support both the mellow and high‑octane moments of the night.
